gigapan.description:
Specimans from Salmon River
ID 2009, 2 of 3
Assorted Taxa 

f22/125th
Macro just under 1.5x
flash at 1/16th power

--
The green patterns in the background are part of my 'chromakey SIFTable pattern.' The idea was to give the stitcher clues, in the form of 
easy to extract SIFT keys from unique(ish) features to give the stitcher solid control points.

Paul Heckbert suggested 'busy font characters.' 

Then once the image stitches I can (in theory :-) treat those colors like a chromakey/greenscreen and remove the pattern. But in reality, I am 
able to identify the macroinvertebrates even with the background, so perhaps it can stay.  So many interesting questions!
